law: To bypass this configuration and use ad hoc signing to test the driver in your local development environment, perform the following steps: Disable System Integrity Protection (SIP) on your system so it recognizes ad hoc-signed DriverKit extensions.
Configure the target to use local signing.
Use the included installer script to install the driver on your system.
This sample code project shows you how to use a tap as an input in a HAL aggregate device, just like a microphone.
To use a tap as an input source, add it to an aggregate device that you configure for playback.
First get the tap’s unique identifier by passing the selector and the tap’s audio object ID to : Then use the selector to get and set the list of taps in an aggregate device.
Before you run the sample code project in Xcode, ensure that you’re using macOS 14.2 or later.
Use a Core Audio tap to capture outgoing audio from a process or group of processes.
Use Audio MIDI Setup to inspect the newly installed device.
